metadata.title: Languedoc – The Taste of France
metadata.description: Authentic journeys in southern France since 2007. Wine, gastronomy and nature with Elisabeth and Morten.

about.title: Elisabeth and Morten
about.lead: We know Languedoc from the inside – and design trips we would take ourselves.
about.body: Since 2007 we have shown guests «proper» southern France – places and people we know personally, with us every step of the way.

stats.recommend: would recommend the trip to a friend
stats.balance: felt the balance between programme and free time was perfect

review1.quote: The trip was complete. 10/10. The place, culture, food and wine – and the vineyard visits were truly excellent.

review2.quote: An experience of the rare kind. No stress, so we could enjoy everything from vines and flowers along the road to restaurant visits you rarely find on your own. Merci beaucoup.

review3.quote: Because you, Morten, are a wonderful and flexible host. You master the role because you know the environment – among wine producers, the local area and as an importer.

review4.quote: It felt like travelling with good friends – just without stress. Small groups, warm people and hospitality that truly cares.
review4.author: Kari and Torbjørn

review5.quote: This comes with our warmest recommendation. Fantastic hospitality, lovely meals, great experiences. 10 out of 10.
review5.author: Kjersti

review6.quote: No stress, no rush – just flow. We had time to breathe, enjoy and actually be present all the way. Balm for the soul.
review6.author: Aslaug

featured.quote: To eat in the south of France is to understand that a meal can be the whole point of the day – and that life is meant to be tasted slowly.

about.title: About us
about.tagline: Two French souls in Norwegian bodies.
about.intro: Over a combined 18 years in Languedoc, friends and acquaintances kept asking us the same question: «Why don't you organise trips for others?» We laughed a little and replied «not quite the right time for that». But the idea stuck – and the desire to share this place grew stronger than we could ignore.
about.elisabeth: Elisabeth is a teacher by background, a tour leader and a cook in practice. She remembers what everyone wants in their glass without asking twice, and has a natural gift for gathering people around a table in a way that helps them relax, laugh and feel they are exactly where they should be. With Elisabeth, no guest is left to fend for themselves.
about.morten: Morten also works with wine and has come to know the region the way only those who have truly let themselves be absorbed by a place can. With Languedoc as his home ground, he knows the producers by name, which cellar hides the vintages nobody knows about, and which restaurant in which village serves the best meal on a Tuesday in May. He brings that knowledge with him – and shares it gladly.
about.together: Together we offer something few travel organisers can: genuine access. Not guided tours behind a rope, but conversations around the producers' own tables and experiences that happen because we know the people behind them.
about.closing: The best trips are not about seeing as much as possible. They are about coming home with a feeling that you were truly somewhere.
about.signoff: Live like a local. Be treated like a guest.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.description: Practical information about trips, booking and your stay.

languedoc.title: Languedoc
languedoc.subtitle: Authentic, warm and a little less polished.
languedoc.intro: Languedoc is something distinct – a region that has spent the last fifty years not preparing for tourists, but cultivating the land and living well. Here you find villages, vineyards, market halls, coast, mountains and architecture with strong stories – without everything feeling packaged like a tourist brochure.

where.title: Where we are
where.body: Our base is in Minervois – one of Languedoc's most exciting wine regions, between the Mediterranean and the Montagne Noire mountain range. The Pyrenees lie to the south. In Minervois, everything is within reach.

stay.title: Where you stay
stay.body: We are a small company that places great emphasis on personal follow-up and quality. Our trips are run in small groups, where closeness makes room for genuine experiences. We have two authentic stone houses from the 1800s – carefully restored to combine historic charm with modern comfort. Original tile floors and hand-forged hinges alongside comfortable beds. When we say you live like a local, it is almost an understatement – here you stay both more spaciously and more comfortably than is usual in the area.

hirondelle.title: L'Hirondelle
hirondelle.body: A classic maison maître from 1820, with high ceilings and an elegance that lives in the walls.

villaBelle.title: Villa Belle
villaBelle.body: A solid stone house from 1850 with thick stone walls, generous shared spaces and a garden that invites long evenings.

stay.closing: Both properties have a pool, garden and patio – and both sit right in the everyday life we want you to experience.

languedoc.statement: Languedoc is where France is still not finished surprising you.
